title: Agentic Chat Eval Harness
creator: Agent Buildprint
summary: A trace-aware, scenario-based Capability Buildprint for grafting a rigorous Agentic Chat evaluation harness into an existing host, with eight profiles, event-sourced eval archive, interactive Eval Operator Console, and deterministic gates behind any model-judge scoring.
promise: Gives coding agents a phased implementation contract for proving whether an existing Agentic Chat runtime behaves correctly under multi-turn, tool, state, routing, UI, harness-runtime, security-governance, and optional RAG scenarios — with archive-backed regression diff and a local interactive operator console.
includes: Agentic Chat eval capability contract with 8 profiles (core-chat, tool-actions, memory-state, provider-routing, ui-proof, rag, harness-runtime, security-governance)
trace-aware scenario harness contract (Scenario, TraceSpan, EvalRun, ScoreResult, EvalReceipt, EvalArchiveEvent, FailureRecord, ConsoleConfig)
eval control plane basis from agentic-eval-evolution-runtime (archive, splits, continual learning loop)
interactive Eval Operator Console phase (Run Explorer, Trace Timeline, Regression Diff, Scenario Debugger, Gate Failure Panel, Negative Proof Runner)
host assessment checkpoint before any source edits
3rd Party Integration Discovery questions for Inspect AI, Braintrust, Ragas/TruLens, tau-bench pattern, OpenAI Evals loop, and hybrid self+adapter paths
phased grafting protocol with 6 implementation phases
compatibility and composition model with agentic-chat
example scenario fixture, eval receipt, archive event, regression diff, and console evidence checklist
research basis with OpenAI Evals, Inspect AI, Braintrust, Ragas, TruLens, and tau-bench references
risks: Evaluates agent behavior that may call tools, mutate state, use private transcripts, trigger external providers, inspect RAG evidence, or produce false confidence about production readiness
Model-judge scoring can reward plausible text despite failed trace or state proof if not bounded by deterministic gates
Test tools may hit production services or perform destructive actions unless sandbox/mocked/blocked
Missing trace hooks create unverified pass claims
RAG profile can pass answer relevance while leaking forbidden context if allow/deny/citation proof is skipped
UI proof claims can hide stuck, fake, or raw debug states behind a success claim
Scenario fixtures drift from production flows if not versioned
Flaky live-model runs can be treated as deterministic regression proof if not bounded
3rd-party integration paths (Inspect AI, Braintrust, Ragas/TruLens, tau-bench pattern) introduce new audit boundaries that must run behind deterministic gates
Console UI must not override deterministic gates or present mock data as proof
checks: Use trace-aware scenario harness; do not grade only the final answer
Capture model, tool, state, retrieval (when present), and final response events in trace spans
Append every run to eval archive with failure record on gate failure
Prove tool/action side effects; sandbox/mock/block destructive tools
Bound model-judge scoring behind deterministic gates
Ship local Eval Operator Console or record explicit console blocker
Prove optional RAG allow, deny, citation, and weak-evidence behavior before claiming RAG coverage
Prove optional UI streaming/action/error/blocked states with screenshot, DOM, or interaction evidence
Write capability receipt with proof level, archive path, console URL, and blockers

trustBadges.label: Guarded capability
trustBadges.detail: Uses phased host assessment, planning, implementation, verification, and receipt.

trustBadges.label: Trace-aware
trustBadges.detail: Scores trace + side effects + state, not just the final answer.

trustBadges.label: Eval control plane
trustBadges.detail: Event-sourced archive, regression diff, and interactive operator console — evals are the control plane.

trustBadges.label: High risk declared
trustBadges.detail: Evaluates agent behavior that may call tools, mutate state, and inspect RAG evidence.

trustBadges.label: 3rd-party optional
trustBadges.detail: Proposes Inspect AI, Braintrust, Ragas/TruLens, tau-bench pattern, and OpenAI Evals loop as deferred adapters behind the existing gates.

trustBadges.label: Locally verifiable
trustBadges.detail: Scenario + archive + receipt fixtures and deterministic gates can prove all eight profiles when host surfaces exist.

publicStatus.label: Draft v1 (publishable)
publicStatus.explanation: Enhanced draft of the Agentic Chat Eval Harness Capability Buildprint. Adds eval control plane basis, event-sourced archive, scenario split tags, interactive Eval Operator Console phase, and console verification gates. Has not yet been applied to a real host with runtime proof; runtime dogfooding receipts will be added under examples/ as they are produced.

whatYouGet: A bounded Agentic Chat eval harness contract
Trace-aware scenario harness with model/tool/state/retrieval/final-response spans
Eight evaluation profiles including harness-runtime, security-governance, and optional RAG grounding
Event-sourced eval archive with last-green baseline and failure record triad
Interactive local Eval Operator Console (Run Explorer, Trace Timeline, Regression Diff, and more)
Deterministic gates behind any model-judge scoring
Phased implementation files (contract, runner, host wiring, scorers, console, verification)
Compatibility and composition model with agentic-chat
Example scenario, receipt, archive event, regression diff, and console evidence checklist
3rd-party integration path discovery (Inspect AI, Braintrust, Ragas/TruLens, tau-bench pattern, OpenAI Evals loop)
whatYouNeed: An existing Agentic Chat host runtime with conversation loop, transcript, and test runner
A traceable tool/action surface with sandbox or mock mode for destructive tools
A provider router or model adapter that can be observed under eval
A host path for serving the local Eval Operator Console (Next.js route, Vite dev server, or static HTML)
An optional RAG retrieval and citation surface if the rag profile is enabled
An optional UI surface if the ui-proof profile is enabled
A regression command that gates future changes
Acceptable cost, latency, and flakiness ceiling for live-model scenarios

howToUse.title: Load the packet
howToUse.detail: Use the package manifest and read BUILDPRINT.md first.

howToUse.title: Answer hard-stop questions
howToUse.detail: Resolve production-critical flows, tool side-effect policy, sandbox strategy, model-judge privacy, RAG privacy, console host path, and CI cost ceiling before implementation.

howToUse.title: Decide 3rd-party integration
howToUse.detail: Run the 3rd Party Integration Discovery in 00-assessment-questions.md. Default path is self-contained (deferred). Adopted paths run behind the existing deterministic gates.

howToUse.title: Plan and graft
howToUse.detail: Write .buildprint/capability-plan.md, then implement each phase in order through the interactive console phase.

howToUse.title: Verify honestly
howToUse.detail: Prove scenario, trace, archive, console, side-effect, optional RAG, and optional UI behavior, then record the actual proof level and integration-path decision.

resultChecklist: Scenario fixtures are versioned, split-tagged, and runnable
Trace spans capture model, tool, state, retrieval (when present), and final response events
Eval archive records every run with failure record triad on gate failure
Deterministic scorers fail on wrong tool calls, missing side effects, unsafe state changes, and missing artifacts
Model-judge scorers are optional, rubric-bound, and never the sole proof for high-risk gates
Interactive Eval Operator Console inspects runs, traces, gate failures, and regression diffs
Receipt records commands, artifacts, archive path, console URL, scenarios, scores, blockers, and claim status
Optional RAG profile proves allow, deny, citation, weak-evidence, and stale/deleted-content behavior before claiming RAG coverage
Optional UI profile proves streaming/action/error/blocked states with screenshot, DOM, or interaction evidence
Destructive tools are sandboxed, mocked, or blocked
Integration path decision is recorded (default: deferred/self-contained)
